# Configure a license

This topic describes how to configure the license so that you can deploy Terraform Enterprise to one of the supported runtimes environments.

You can provide either a HashiCorp License or an IBM Passport Advantage Online (PAO) license. Use a HashiCorp license if you have a legacy HashiCorp license key. Use a PAO license if you purchased Terraform Enterprise through IBM Passport Advantage Online.


## Apply the Terraform Enterprise license

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HashiCorp License" group="hashicorp">

There are two ways to apply the new license file on startup:

- Provide the body of the license file as a string to an environment variable called `TFE_LICENSE`.
- Store the license in a file and provide the file path to an environment variable called `TFE_LICENSE_PATH`.
- For Kubernetes and OpenShift deployments, you can also store the installation key as a Kubernetes secret and map it to `TFE_LICENSE` through your Helm values. Refer to [Deploy Terraform Enterprise to Kubernetes](/terraform/enterprise/deploy/kubernetes) or [Deploy Terraform Enterprise to OpenShift](/terraform/enterprise/deploy/openshift) for runtime-specific secret configuration guidance.

</Tab>
<Tab heading="IBM Passport Advantage Online" group="pao">

If you purchased Terraform Enterprise through IBM Passport Advantage, you must obtain your credentials from IBM Passport Advantage Online. The PAO portal provides the following credentials:

- Installation key: This installation key is equivalent to the HashiCorp license and is configured through the `TFE_LICENSE` environment variable.
- Access key: A credential for authenticating with the IBM Entitled Registry at `cp.icr.io` when pulling the Terraform Enterprise container image.

<Note>
As opposed to the HashiCorp license, the installation key cannot authenticate with any container registry. You must use the separate access key to pull the Terraform Enterprise image from the IBM Entitled Registry.
</Note>

1. Log in to the [Passport Advantage website](https://www.ibm.com/software/passportadvantage).
1. Under **Software download**, select **Download Software**.
1. Locate **IBM Terraform Self-Managed Premium** in your list of software downloads, then click **View Product**.
1. Continue to the specific product version.
1. Click **Download key** under the **Installation Key** section to download your license key.

Apply the license key using one of the following methods:

- Set the `TFE_LICENSE` environment variable to the contents of the installation key:

   ```shell-session
   $ export TFE_LICENSE="<INSTALLATION_KEY>"
   ```

- Store the license key in a file and set the `TFE_LICENSE_PATH` environment variable to the file path:

   ```shell-session
   $ export TFE_LICENSE_PATH="/path/to/license-key"
   ```

- For Kubernetes and OpenShift deployments, you can also store the installation key as a Kubernetes secret and map it to `TFE_LICENSE` through your Helm values. Refer to [Deploy Terraform Enterprise to Kubernetes](/terraform/enterprise/deploy/kubernetes) or [Deploy Terraform Enterprise to OpenShift](/terraform/enterprise/deploy/openshift) for runtime-specific secret configuration guidance.

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## License expiration and termination

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="HashiCorp License"  group="hashicorp">

To prevent unexpected outages caused by delays in license updates, licenses provisioned for use in production don't terminate on their expiry date. If your license key expires, active Terraform Enterprise instances continue to operate. You must update the key with a valid license in order to restart a deployment or to install a new deployment.

If your license expires, you cannot use it for authentication with the HashiCorp image registry at `images.registry.hashicorp.com`. If you configure your installation to pull directly from the HashiCorp registry and your license expires, you cannot reinstall, scale, or upgrade.

To check your license expiration date, use the [`tfectl app license` command](/terraform/enterprise/deploy/reference/cli#review-hashicorp-license-status).

</Tab>
<Tab heading="IBM Passport Advantage Online"  group="pao">

To prevent unexpected outages caused by delays in license updates, licenses provisioned for use in production don't terminate on their expiry date. If your license key expires, active Terraform Enterprise instances continue to operate. You must update the key with a valid license in order to restart a deployment or to install a new deployment.

The IBM Entitled Registry only allows access if your container registry access key is active. To access images, ensure your access key remains active in IBM Passport Advantage Online.

To check your license expiration date, use the [`tfectl app license` command](/terraform/enterprise/deploy/reference/cli#review-hashicorp-license-status).

</Tab>
</Tabs>

## License updates

To update an existing installation with a new license, you will need to modify the `TFE_LICENSE` or `TFE_LICENSE_PATH`
variable and restart the application.
